other_observers: solo; found on 09/20/06 by Brian Gibbons  see on later dates by  Brandon Percival Nick Komar Andrew Spencer John Drummond
specific_location: at the Wilson Ranch 100 yds north of the intersection of CR 1 and Rd KK. The Ranch is on the Pueblo side of the road. The bird also perched on the power line which runs along the east side of the road which is in Crowley County.
description: Actually this isn't so critical because it is a straightforward I.D. The very long deeply forked tail and pink underparts with the gray head and back and dark wings, eye and bill make the choice only Scissor-tailed Flycatcher. The tail has a white edge on the outer rectrices with black tips. The tips almost looked similar to those of most motmots species but were not quite racketed. The sex of the bird is really an unknown, but I was leaning towards a short-tailed male.Behaviors: Active. Perched 4 times on various open perches including powerlines, guy wires, and fence wires. Also seen perched in a plains cottonwood tree. At times actively flycatching at which time it arched its tail up, spread out to show the deep fork in it.Call: calls heard sounded like pik pik and also kapikPlumage: alternate
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
